content.full_text:
84294 atag
84214

May 31, 1977

77-03-181

Mr. L. B. Thomas
City Clerk
Post Office Box 660
Pomona, California 91769

Dear Mr. Thomas:

Thank you for your letter of May 25, 1977 concerning the filing
obligations of a defeated candidate for office who is receiving
or expending funds in connection with the candidacy after the
post-election campaign statement has been filed in connection
with the election for which he was a candidate.

The candidate maintains an obligation to file campaign statements
on an off-year basis as long as there is an activity to report
in connection with a previous candidacy.

The "termination" procedure outlined in our bulletin refers to
recipient committees only and not candidates. However, even
recipient committees may not terminate if they are still receiving
or expending funds.

Please let me know if I may be of additional assistance.

Very truly yours,


Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator

RSK:bw

24

LAWRENCE B. THOMAS

City Clerk

THE CITY OF POMONA

Box 660, Pomona, CA 91769 (714) 620-2341

Office of the City Clerk

May 25, 1977


Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804

Attention:  Technical Assistance and Analysis Division

Gentlemen:

Does a candidate who is defeated for election in March, and after
filing his final campaign statement has an unpaid liability, have
to file an additional statement when the liability is paid; or
does his obligation to file terminate at the end of this calendar
year as set forth in your bulletin dated April 15, 1977.

Sincerely yours,

L. B. THOMAS
City Clerk

LBT:fp

sections.question: Does a candidate who is defeated for election in March, and after filing his final campaign statement has an unpaid liability, have to file an additional statement when the liability is paid; or does his obligation to file terminate at the end of this calendar year as set forth in your bulletin dated April 15, 1977.
sections.question_synthetic: A defeated candidate who filed a final campaign statement in March 1977 has an unpaid liability. The question asks whether an additional statement must be filed when the liability is paid, or whether the filing obligation terminates at year-end per FPPC bulletin dated April 15, 1977.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section present. Document contains clear question section. Some text degradation in header area but main question text is legible. Sender is City Clerk requesting technical assistance.

embedding.summary: This is a letter from the City Clerk of Pomona, California, dated May 25, 1977, requesting clarification from the FPPC regarding campaign statement filing obligations for a defeated candidate with outstanding liabilities.
